response.justification: Candidate has 14+ years of strong software engineering experience, but the profile is heavily web/full-stack (JavaScript/TypeScript, Node.js, DLang) with only a brief ~6-month C++ stint at National Instruments over a decade ago. The role demands current, deep C++/STL expertise plus industrial automation, MQTT, IEC 62443 SDL, and message-broker architectures — none of which appear in the resume. Transferable skills exist (distributed systems, low-latency services, IoT-adjacent work), but the core technical and domain gaps are too large for a Mid-Senior C++ industrial automation position.
strengths: 14+ years of professional software engineering, including low-latency, high-throughput micro-services at Dunnhumby
Solid distributed/event-driven systems exposure and full-stack range across web, mobile, desktop, embedded
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, mentoring, workshops, and open-source contributions
concerns: No substantive recent C++/STL experience — only a short C/C++ role in 2014–2015
No evidence of industrial automation, MQTT, IEC 62443, SDL, or industrial protocols (Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850)
No demonstrated secure-development-lifecycle or industrial cybersecurity background

key_factors.factor: No industrial automation / IEC 62443 / SDL background
Strong general engineering and distributed-systems experience
